text: The Black Start Unit shall initiate startup without any source of off-site electric power.
The Black Start Unit shall deliver energy to the grid without assistance from the electrical system.
The Black Start Unit shall maintain frequency within +/- 0.1 Hz during islanded restoration operations.
The Black Start Unit shall maintain voltage within emergency voltage limits from no load to full external load.
The Black Start Unit shall utilize an isochronous governor during system restoration.
The Hydroelectric Unit shall synchronize to the grid within 30 minutes of initiation.
The Combustion Gas Turbine shall synchronize to the grid within 30 minutes of initiation.
The Industrial Gas Turbine shall synchronize to the grid within 60 minutes of initiation.
The Hot Steam-Driven Turbine shall synchronize to the grid within 150 minutes of initiation.
The Black Start Test shall maintain a minimum synchronized generation duration of 10 minutes.
The Black Start Unit shall produce reactive power resources as specified in the RMR contract voltage support section.
The Black Start Unit shall maintain 99% of the Requested MW during the four-hour availability test.
The Generator Protection System shall remain operational during abnormal grid restoration conditions.
The Unit Owner shall notify the CAISO RT Dispatcher by phone within 24 hours of conducting a test.
The Unit Owner shall file a formal letter with CAISO within 14 days of test completion.
The Unit Owner shall submit ambient temperature data to CAISO by 11:00 the next business day after a test.
The CAISO shall annually verify that the location and size of units meet WECC restoration requirements.
The CAISO shall document Cranking Paths between each Black Start unit and the units to be started.
The Voluntary Unit Owner shall demonstrate performance at least every five years through simulation or testing.
The CAISO shall maintain a database including unit name, location, capacity, type, and latest test date.
The Real-time Grid Operators shall receive annual training for system restoration using black start units.
The CAISO shall review and update the Black Start Capability Plan at least every five years.
The Participant shall submit a CAISO market schedule for any energy generated during a Black Start test.
The CAISO shall not request a Black Start Test for a hydroelectric Unit during periods of water constraint.
The Black Start Test should include SCADA and telephone communications as primary operating aids.
The Unit Owner shall provide a corrective action plan within 30 days if the unit fails the testing criteria.
